cases.name: Client wants 'unaudited statements with no opinion' — preparation vs compilation routing

intake.user_request: Client needs unaudited financial statements for their bank. Bank says 'no opinion required'. Should we do a preparation engagement or a compilation?

cases.expected_conclusion: Bank language ('no opinion required') is ambiguous. If the bank requires the firm to issue a report (even one disclaiming an opinion), use AR-C 80 (compilation) with the standard compilation report. If the bank only requires F/S without a CPA report and accepts a 'no assurance is provided' legend on each page, AR-C 70 (preparation) is appropriate. Confirm with the bank in writing. If they require any CPA report, AR-C 70 is not available — every page-legend approach precludes any CPA report. Engagement letter required before work begins (AR-C 60.A21 + 70.10 or 80.10).

cases.name: Review engagement with going-concern uncertainty

going_concern_indicators: debt covenant violation in current year
loss of major customer 35% of revenue
intake.user_request: Mid-review of nonissuer manufacturing client. Multiple going-concern indicators. Management's footnote disclosure mentions 'cyclical industry headwinds' but does not address ASC 205-40 substantial doubt. What modifications to the review report are required?

cases.expected_conclusion: AR-C 90.65 requires the practitioner to evaluate going concern consistent with the framework (ASC 205-40). The indicators (recurring losses, covenant violation, major-customer loss) collectively raise substantial doubt about the entity's ability to continue as a going concern within one year of the F/S issuance date. Required procedures: (1) inquire of management about plans to mitigate; (2) evaluate whether plans are probable of being effectively implemented and probable of mitigating the conditions. If substantial doubt exists and management's footnote does NOT meet ASC 205-40 disclosure requirements, the review report must be modified — qualified conclusion (or adverse if pervasive) and an emphasis-of-matter paragraph should not substitute for adequate disclosure. Alternatively, request management to revise the footnote to meet ASC 205-40 (specifically address principal conditions, management's evaluation, and substantial-doubt conclusion). If management refuses revision and the disclosure is materially inadequate, modify the review conclusion. Withdraw if dispute is irreconcilable.

cases.name: Long-time bookkeeping client requests compilation; firm is not independent

intake.user_request: Firm has done outsourced bookkeeping for client for 10 years (firm posts all entries, including period-end accruals — this is management participation under §1.295). Client now needs compiled tax-basis F/S for new line of credit. Bank accepts compilation report with disclosure of lack of independence.

cases.expected_conclusion: Compilation under AR-C 80 with AR-C 80.27 lack-of-independence disclosure paragraph is permitted. Required steps: (1) signed engagement letter per AR-C 60.A21 + 80.10 stating tax-basis framework, lack of independence, and disclosure; (2) read F/S for obvious errors and form-appropriateness; (3) issue compilation report with the standard compilation paragraph PLUS a paragraph stating '[I am / We are] not independent with respect to [Client]' — practitioner may but is not required to disclose specific reasons (must disclose ALL substantive reasons if any are disclosed); (4) lack-of-independence path is NOT available for review (AR-C 90 requires independence); cannot route to AR-C 90. Recommend separately: address the underlying §1.295 management-participation finding so future engagements have a path to review or audit when bank requirements escalate.
